sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_array_reverse (id Int32, values Array(Int32), value_score Array(Int32), str_values Array(String));
INSERT INTO test_array_reverse VALUES (2, [nan, 2, NULL, 3, 1], [2, NULL, 7, 3, 1], ['alpha', NULL, 'delta', 'gamma', 'beta']);
CREATE TABLE reference_score (id Int32, reference Array(Int32));
SELECT id, arrayReverseSort(values, value_score) AS sorted_values FROM test_array_reverse WHERE arrayReverseSort(value_score) BETWEEN 0 AND 5;
SELECT id, arrayReverseSort((x, y) -> y, str_values, value_score) AS sorted_strings FROM test_array_reverse WHERE 3 BETWEEN (arrayReverseSort(value_score)[1]) AND (arrayReverseSort(value_score)[2]);

SELECT id, arrayReverseSort((x, y) -> y, values, value_score) AS sorted_array FROM test_array_reverse WHERE (VALUES[1] BETWEEN 1 AND 3) AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reference_score WHERE reference[0] >= 10);

SELECT r.id, r.reference, t.id FROM reference_score r JOIN (SELECT id, arrayReverseSort(values, value_score) AS sorted_array FROM test_array_reverse) t ON (t.sorted_array[1] BETWEEN r.reference[0] AND r.reference[1]);
SELECT id, arrayReverseSort((x, y) -> x <= y, values, value_score) AS final_sort FROM test_array_reverse HAVING final_sort IS NOT NULL;
